response.justification: Candidate has ~16 years of solid full-stack software engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems, with demonstrated craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, OSS founding). However, the role is a Director-level IC Principal Engineer in AI strategy, and the resume shows only recent, light exposure to AI work (MCP code generators at current role) with no demonstrated AI/ML products, platforms, research, or strategic advisory experience. There is also no evidence of Principal/Director-level scope, cloud-platform strategy work, or advising strategic customers on cutting-edge AI adoption.
strengths: ~16 years of broad engineering experience across full stack, embedded, and high-throughput microservices
Demonstrated software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, OSS test tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial)
Entrepreneurial track record — founded and sustains GISCollective open-source GIS platform with real users
concerns: Minimal demonstrated AI/ML depth; no shipped AI products, platforms, or research to substantiate 'recognized AI expertise'
Career level reads as Senior/Staff IC; no evidence of Principal/Director scope, org-wide influence, or external thought leadership at that tier
Limited cloud-platform strategy experience — AWS CDK usage at one role, but no architecture/strategy work with strategic customers or partners

key_factors.factor: Lack of demonstrated AI expertise and AI products
Seniority gap vs. Director-level IC scope
Long, varied engineering tenure with craftsmanship focus
